Here's what I found across PubMed. Solid set of recent papers on neural coupling in music:

---

## Literature Search: Neural Coupling in Music

### 🔬 Key Papers (2024–2026)

**1. Distinct social dynamics of joint action represented by interpersonal neural coupling in congruent and incongruent joint musical performances**
- **Authors:** Di Yuan et al.
- **Journal:** NeuroImage (2026 Jan; 325:121662)
- **DOI:** [10.1016/j.neuroimage.2025.121662](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2025.121662)
- **TLDR:** Recorded 50 pairs of string players via EEG hyperscanning during unison vs. melody-accompaniment performance. Found that PFC supports congruent (unison) actions via action prediction, while left TPJ supports incongruent (complementary) actions via self-other differentiation. Melody players led accompanists in INC during incongruent performances.
- **Key insight:** Incongruent performances were more demanding and showed greater audience-performer consensus — social significance of complementary roles in music.

**2. Unveiling the Neural Signature of Group Cohesion in Music Co-Audition**
- **Authors:** Ying Ding et al.
- **Journal:** Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences (2025 Dec; 1554(1):203-215)
- **DOI:** [10.1111/nyas.70105](https://doi.org/10.1111/nyas.70105)
- **TLDR:** Measured EEG and continuous emotional ratings while groups listened to music together. Found neural signatures linking shared musical experience to social closeness — provides a neurobehavioral framework for understanding group cohesion through shared music.

**3. Environmental effects on inter-brain coupling: a systematic review**
- **Authors:** Leahy O, Kontaris E, Gunasekara N, Hirsch J, Tachtsidis I
- **Journal:** Frontiers in Human Neuroscience (2025 Jul; 19:1627457)
- **DOI:** [10.3389/fnhum.2025.1627457](https://doi.org/10.3389/fnhum.2025.1627457) — Free PMC
- **TLDR:** Systematic review showing IBC is significantly modulated by environmental factors including interpersonal distance, background noise, VR, and **music**. Music emerged as a key environmental modulator of neural synchrony in social cognition regions.

**4. Hyper-brain hyper-frequency network topology dynamics when playing guitar in quartet**
- **Authors:** Müller V, Lindenberger U
- **Journal:** Frontiers in Human Neuroscience (2024 Jun; 18:1416667)
- **DOI:** [10.3389/fnhum.2024.1416667](https://doi.org/10.3389/fnhum.2024.1416667) — Free PMC
- **TLDR:** Constructed hyper-brain hyper-frequency networks (HB-HFNs) from a guitar quartet using within- and cross-frequency coupling. Shows how ensemble interaction manifests as network topology dynamics — pushing hyperscanning methodology beyond simple pairwise synchrony.

**5. Non-verbal joint action in healthy adults: a systematic scoping review of EEG-hyperscanning research**
- **Authors:** De Fano A, Fiedler P, Zappasodi F, Bertollo M, Comani S
- **Journal:** Social Cognitive and Affective Neuroscience (2025; 20(1):nsaf050)
- **DOI:** [10.1093/scan/nsaf050](https://doi.org/10.1093/scan/nsaf050) — Free PMC
- **TLDR:** Reviewed 39 EEG-hyperscanning studies on non-verbal joint action (music, movement, computer tasks). Music-based tasks are prominent paradigms. Phase synchrony and graph theory are the dominant INC measures. Cooperative/simultaneous actions are well-studied; competitive/turn-based remain underexplored.

---

### 📊 Notable Older Papers (2022–2023)

| Paper | Authors | Journal | Year | Key Finding |
|-------|---------|---------|------|-------------|
| Audience IBS during live music | Chabin T et al. | Front Hum Neurosci | 2022 | IBS scales with shared pleasure intensity and number of people sharing it |
| Interbrain sync in piano duets | Lender A, Müller V et al. | Ann NY Acad Sci | 2023 | Dynamic IBS patterns during pianists' sensorimotor coordination |
| Brain-to-brain in musical improvisation | Ramírez-Moreno MA et al. | F1000Research | 2023 | More musicians → more synchronized EEG, especially temporal-parietal-occipital |
| EEG IBS in music therapy (children w/ disabilities) | Kang K et al. | Eur J Neurosci | 2023 | Music therapy sessions showed higher IBS than non-music; clinical implications for ASD/CP |
| Guitarist quartet + audience concert | Müller V, Lindenberger U | Ann NY Acad Sci | 2023 | Multilevel IBS between performers and audience during live concert |

---

### 🧭 Themes & Takeaways

1. **Hyperscanning is the dominant method** — EEG hyperscanning (dual/multi-brain recording) is the workhorse for studying neural coupling in music
2. **PFC vs TPJ distinction** — PFC for action prediction in synchronized play, TPJ for self-other differentiation in complementary roles (Yuan et al., 2026)
3. **Audience coupling is real** — not just performer-performer; shared music listening produces measurable IBS modulated by emotional engagement and social closeness
4. **Clinical applications emerging** — music therapy with children (Kang et al., 2023) shows promising IBS-based biomarkers
5. **Methodological evolution** — from simple phase synchrony → graph theory → hyper-frequency networks (Müller & Lindenberger pushing the frontier)

---

This is very much in your wheelhouse given your work on speaker-listener neural coupling 🧠🎵 The Yuan et al. 2026 paper on congruent vs. incongruent joint performance is probably the most directly relevant — want me to dig deeper into any of these?
